title: gonystylus areolatus
intro: Gonystylus areolatus, also known as the Kelat tree, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, and its bark, leaves, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, among other health benefits. Gonystylus areolatus is traditionally prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
